insights: Comprehensive data access layer with 760 lines of well-documented code
Implements full CRUD operations with tenant isolation via organizationId
Uses Prisma ORM with strong type safety through TypeScript interfaces
Enforces soft delete pattern through notDeleted filter on all queries
Provides both basic and advanced query methods (findById, findBySlug, findByIdWithRelations)
Includes pagination support with normalized perPage limits (1-100)
Implements search functionality with case-insensitive multi-field searching
Provides convenience methods like findByIdOrThrow and findBySlugOrThrow
Includes summary queries optimized for UI dropdowns with count aggregations
Parallel query execution for performance (count and find in Promise.all)
Defines clear interfaces for input/output (CreateMenuData, UpdateMenuData, MenuSummary)
Service layer providing business logic abstraction over repository
Uses dependency injection with tsyringe for MenuRepository
Implements facade pattern to simplify repository complexity for consumers
Provides high-level operations like publish, unpublish with business rules
Includes validation and error handling with AppException
Manages slug generation and uniqueness validation
Coordinates multi-step operations (e.g., publishing with timestamp)
Uses transactions for consistency where needed
Provides convenient wrapper methods matching repository operations
Handles default menu logic and published menu queries

patterns_discovered.description: Separation of data access (MenuRepository) from business logic (MenuService) for maintainability and testability
All queries require organizationId to enforce multi-tenant data segregation at the database layer
Uses notDeleted filter on all queries instead of hard deletion, preserving data integrity
Uses tsyringe @injectable and @inject decorators for loose coupling and container management
Uses AppException.notFound() for consistent error handling with standard exception hierarchy
Normalizes pagination parameters (page, perPage) with min/max constraints to prevent abuse
Provides dual methods (findById/findByIdOrThrow) for flexible error handling strategies
Uses Prisma's _count and groupBy for efficient nested resource counting without N+1 queries
Uses Promise.all() for count and find operations to improve pagination performance

gotchas_discovered.description: Slug uniqueness is scoped to organization, allowing same slug across different organizations - requires explicit validation in service layer
Product counts in MenuSummary require separate groupBy query on categories table - potential performance consideration for large datasets
Page/perPage values are silently normalized rather than throwing errors - could mask incorrect client requests
findDefaultMenu assumes only one default menu exists - no enforcement of uniqueness constraint at repository level
All queries must remember to include notDeleted filter - easy to forget in custom query additions, leading to data leakage
Theme relationship is lazy-loaded in basic queries but included in relations queries - service must handle both cases

approach_outcome.description: Successfully implemented a production-ready Menu service and repository layer
key_achievements: Complete CRUD operations with comprehensive query methods
Tenant-isolated data access with enforced soft deletes
Performance optimizations including pagination and parallel queries
Extensive JSDoc documentation with code examples
Summary queries optimized for UI consumption
approach_outcome.code_quality: High - well-documented, type-safe, follows established patterns
approach_outcome.test_readiness: Ready for unit and integration testing with clear method contracts

recommendations.rationale: Implement unique constraint on (organizationId, slug) and unique constraint on (organizationId, isDefault=true) to enforce business rules at database level
Create reusable slug generator and validator to ensure consistency across menus and prevent invalid slug patterns
Monitor the nested count aggregation in findAllSummaries - consider caching or denormalization if called frequently
Test all CRUD operations, pagination edge cases, tenant isolation, and soft delete behavior
Test business logic like publish/unpublish, default menu management, and multi-step operations
Add Redis caching for frequently accessed queries like findDefaultMenu and findPublished
Use Zod or similar for runtime validation of Prisma results to catch schema mismatches early
